title: quercus sessiliflora subsp. kerneri
intro: Quercus sessiliflora subsp. kerneri is a subspecies of evergreen tree that belongs to the Quercus genus and is native to the Mediterranean region. It is also known as Kermes oak, a tree species that has been utilized for its medicinal properties for centuries. The leaves of Quercus sessiliflora subsp. kerneri contain flavonoids, phenolic acids, and other bioactive compounds, which have been found to exhibit anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. Traditionally, the leaves have been used to prepare herbal remedies, such as teas and infusions, which are said to help lower blood pressure, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues.
